```json
[
  {
    "idea": "Unemployment Claims Spike Reversal Trade",
    "family": "labor_jobs",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series ICSA (Initial Claims) via fred_series adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Weekly jobless claims spike above 4-week moving average by >15%, historically followed by 1-2 week relief rally as traders price in temporary shock.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Consumer discretionary rallies on claims relief after initial panic, as jobs data stabilizes expectations."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If ICSA > (4-week MA + 15%), enter long XLY",
      "exit": "After 7 trading days OR if ICSA drops below 2-week MA",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Claims data is weekly; seasonal volatility and economic noise ensure 2–3 spikes per month above threshold."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Truck Tonnage Drop-Off Signal",
    "family": "freight_logistics",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series TRUCKD (Truck Tonnage Index) via fred_series adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Truck tonnage falls >3% week-over-week, signaling logistics slowdown. Historically mean-reverts within 2–3 weeks as demand reasserts.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Industrial/transport stocks fall on demand shock; rebound trades when tonnage stabilizes."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If TRUCKD drops >3% WoW and 5-day RSI < 35, short XLI",
      "exit": "After 10 trading days OR if TRUCKD posts 2 consecutive WoW gains",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Weekly tonnage data swings >3% regularly due to holiday, seasonal, and shipping-cycle volatility."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Natural Gas Demand Weather Whip",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series DHHNGSP (Henry Hub Natural Gas Spot Price) + Open-Meteo daily temperature anomaly",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "When US avg temperature drops >2 standard deviations below normal and gas prices lag spot moves, heating demand surge is priced in slowly. Utilities and energy stocks get whipsawed.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLU",
      "rationale": "Utilities benefit from heating demand; price reaction lags weather, creating mean-reversion entry."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If daily US avg temp anomaly < -2 SD and XLU underperforms XLP by >1.5% over 3 days, long XLU",
      "exit": "After 5 trading days OR once temp anomaly crosses back to -1 SD",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Winter/early spring weather swings >2 SD occur multiple times per month; daily data fires frequently."
  },
  {
    "idea": "EV Charger Buildout Frenzy Signal",
    "family": "local_economy_weirdness",
    "data": {
      "source": "OpenChargeMap public charger count API via openchargemap adapter, aggregated by state weekly",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Weekly net additions of public EV chargers in top 5 states surge >20% above 12-week rolling average, signaling infrastructure grant deployment or corporate spending wave.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLE",
      "rationale": "EV buildout indirectly bullish for energy infrastructure, materials (XLB), and industrial (XLI) as capex ramps."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly net charger adds > (12-week MA + 20%), long XLI (industrial/materials suppliers)",
      "exit": "After 14 trading days OR if adds fall below 12-week MA for 2 consecutive weeks",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Infrastructure spending cycles and seasonal capex deployment ensure 2–3 spikes per quarter above threshold."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Airline Load Factor Collapse-Rebound",
    "family": "travel_mobility",
    "data": {
      "source": "BTS airline load factor weekly via bts_airline_load_factor adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Weekly load factor drops >2.5 percentage points from prior week (e.g., 82% to 79.5%), signaling demand shock. Historically bounces within 3 weeks as pricing adjusts.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Airlines and hospitality compress margins; consumer discretionary sentiment falls on travel weakness."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If load factor WoW drop > 2.5 ppts, short XLY for 7 trading days",
      "exit": "After 7 days OR if load factor rebounds >1.5 ppts WoW",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal demand swings, holidays, and fuel/economic shocks cause load factor whipsaws weekly."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Container Port Volume Spike-Drop Friction Trade",
    "family": "freight_logistics",
    "data": {
      "source": "Port container volume aggregate (Port of Long Beach, Port of NY/NJ) via port_container_volume adapter, weekly",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Weekly container volume (TEU) swings >8% from 4-week MA signal logistics bottleneck or surge. Retail/industrial stocks gap on shipping friction expectations.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLI",
      "rationale": "Sharp volume swings signal supply-chain friction; industrial and materials stocks react negatively to logistics volatility."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If weekly TEU > (4-week MA + 8%) OR < (4-week MA - 8%), short XLI",
      "exit": "After 10 trading days OR if TEU returns within 3% of 4-week MA",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal shipping cycles, holidays, and demand shocks drive ±8% swings monthly."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Healthcare Cost Shock Google Search Spike Trade",
    "family": "consumer_stress",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly search volume for 'medical bill debt' + 'insurance deductible shock' via google_trends adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Combined search volume for medical cost distress terms spikes >40% above 12-week MA, indicating acute consumer stress. Staples and defensive stocks rally as discretionary demand falters.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLP",
      "rationale": "Consumer stress rotation favors staples; healthcare cost anxiety is sticky and reverses slowly, supporting defensive positioning."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If combined search volume > (12-week MA + 40%), long XLP",
      "exit": "After 14 trading days OR if search volume falls below 12-week MA",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal billing cycles, insurance deductible resets, and healthcare premium announcements cause monthly spikes."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Earthquake Activity Precursor Insurance & Construction Volatility",
    "family": "local_economy_weirdness",
    "data": {
      "source": "USGS earthquake activity magnitude >4.0 US daily count via earthquake_activity adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "daily",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Daily count of US earthquakes >4.0 magnitude spikes >3 days running above 12-month average, triggering insurance & construction cost anxiety. Financials (insurance) and materials (rebuilding) gap.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLF",
      "rationale": "Elevated seismic activity increases insurer loss reserves and risk perception; financial sector reprices catastrophe risk."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If 3-day rolling count of mag >4.0 events > 12-month daily avg, short XLF",
      "exit": "After 7 trading days OR if 7-day rolling count drops below 12-month avg",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "US seismic zones (California, Oklahoma, Pacific NW) experience clusters monthly; daily data ensures frequent firing."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Fed Liquidity Drain Week Volatility Squeeze",
    "family": "macro_input_pressure",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series WALCL (Federal Reserve Assets, Liabilities) via fred_series adapter, weekly change",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Weekly Fed balance sheet shrinkage >$5B signals liquidity drain. Historically correlates with intraweek volatility spike and defensive sector outperformance.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bullish",
      "sector": "XLU",
      "rationale": "Liquidity tightening favors stable dividend payers (utilities) over growth stocks; flight to safety."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If WALCL weekly change < -$5B, long XLU vs. XLK spread (long XLU, short XLK)",
      "exit": "After 5 trading days OR if WALCL rebounds >$2B WoW",
      "expected_frequency": "high"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "weekly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Fed quantitative tightening is structural; weekly >$5B drains occur regularly during rate-hike cycles."
  },
  {
    "idea": "Bank Lending Standards Tightening Signal",
    "family": "consumer_stress",
    "data": {
      "source": "FRED series LMNRNRNR (Banks Tightening Lending Standards Net Percentage) via fred_series adapter, quarterly proxy to weekly moving average",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "exact_frequency_note": "Quarterly release, interpolated to weekly trend",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Lending standards tighten sharply (spike in net % tightening), signaling credit shock. Consumer discretionary and small-cap indices fall as credit availability shrinks.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLY",
      "rationale": "Tight lending standards crush consumer credit-dependent spending; discretionary retail and finance compress."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "If net tightening % > (prior 2-quarter avg + 15 ppts), short XLY for 14 days",
      "exit": "After 14 trading days OR if tightening reverses >10 ppts in next quarterly release",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 24,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "medium",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Quarterly Fed surveys release, interpolated weekly; 2–3 sharp tightening shocks per year align with credit events."
  }
]
```
